title: rosa canina subsp. catalaunica
intro: Rosa canina subsp. catalaunica, also known as the wild rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. It is traditionally valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and skin problems, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The health benefits of Rosa canina subsp. catalaunica include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of diarrhea, stomach ulcers, and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote digestive health and support overall well-being.
